Dave Goldblatt 10/24/2024 7:56 PM

Dave Goldblatt
what is the plan for a quantum bio dao token? (if any?)
Clarice D. Aiello 10/24/2024 8:45 PM
Calling @Marinewolf !!!
1
Dave Goldblatt
what is the plan for a quantum bio dao token? (if any?)
Marinewolf 10/24/2024 9:32 PM
There will be a token! We are currently still in the early stages of mapping out tokenomics, but they'll follow the general contour of other bioDAOs (hairDAO, VitaDAO, etc) Do you have any opinions on tokens or tokenomics you'd like to share?
1
Dave Goldblatt 10/25/2024 1:21 PM
i have thoughts more generally, but im not an expert on in depth tokenomics
1:24 PM
tldr "instant" liquidity via a token is a double edged sword. these things are essentially "seed" venture type private assets, and as such there isn't much market information on a price. by making the asset liquid and priced, with much more market information, you introduce a flood of capital (and everything that is good along with it), but also suffer the downsides of having to manage public perception tied to a quantifiable dollar value
2
1:25 PM
what the "correct" thing is i don't know, but in general i prefer the middle path of not too degen but just enough to have the option value to generate fomo, momentum, and liquidity to drive progress at an accelerated rate
1
Dave Goldblatt
what the "correct" thing is i don't know, but in general i prefer the middle path of not too degen but just enough to have the option value to generate fomo, momentum, and liquidity to drive progress at an accelerated rate
Marinewolf 10/25/2024 4:12 PM
completely agree! we are a scientific ecosystem first, too degen would scare away the scientsts (who are ultimately our customers!)
1
4:15 PM
we will be sure to update the community once a plan on tokenomics comes together. it's really important for us to find consensus with the community on this because we want to be a network!

Waves 11/15/2024 6:23 PM
What's the point on holding QuantumBioDAO token if it is a non-profit?
Waves
What's the point on holding QuantumBioDAO token if it is a non-profit?
Clarice D. Aiello 11/15/2024 7:55 PM
@Geoff will give you details, the Institute is a non-profit; the DAO is a Swiss foundation that can hold IP. There's an interplay of our 3 institutions, which will become clear in the white paper we're preparing. For example, see the roadmap https://www.quantumbiology.eco/roadmap The DAO will generate IP; and the DAO will own a stake at the Incubator. (edited)
Quantum Biology Ecosystem | Roadmap
The Quantum Biology Ecosystem is a collective of organizations synergistically building the quantum biology industry by design.
Geoff 11/15/2024 7:58 PM
Actually, the DAO is a Swiss foundation and will be a non-profit. The point of the token is governance — people who hold the token will be able to vote on and direct the funds in the DAO to different research projects.
1
Geoff
Actually, the DAO is a Swiss foundation and will be a non-profit. The point of the token is governance — people who hold the token will be able to vote on and direct the funds in the DAO to different research projects.
Waves 11/15/2024 9:53 PM
So, from a speculative standpoint (for us 'greedy token holders'), there is no point in holding the DAO token, as it offers no share in the Seeded DAO/Incubation/IP, right ?
Waves
So, from a speculative standpoint (for us 'greedy token holders'), there is no point in holding the DAO token, as it offers no share in the Seeded DAO/Incubation/IP, right ?
Clarice D. Aiello 11/15/2024 10:09 PM
@Waves, all Bio.xyz DAOs (e.g., VitaDAO) are Swiss foundations. The $QBIO token will offer governance rights in Incubation and IP, just like all other Bio.xyz DAOs. @Geoff @Marinewolf can give you more details. (edited)

pyramidmaker 11/18/2024 5:01 AM
@Clarice D. Aiello is the research affiliated to MIT at all? is there an agreement between the foundation and MIT at all? or is it completely outside of any university?
pyramidmaker
@Clarice D. Aiello is the research affiliated to MIT at all? is there an agreement between the foundation and MIT at all? or is it completely outside of any university?
Clarice D. Aiello 11/18/2024 7:53 AM
We have collaborators at MIT-Lincoln Labs. The research performed by the Institute is not affiliated with an University. We have assessed that this model makes the science go faster, in addition to being free from other academic constraints– for example, we perform radical open science, with all materials freely available, like we already did with our first result, https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2024.10.10.617626v2 . We intend to radicalize the concept by streaming our weekly group meetings where Institute staff share their weekly progress.
Weak magnetic field effects in biology are measurable—accelerated X...
Despite decades of reports of weak magnetic field effects in biology across the tree of life and on a broad range of cell types, the evidence to date remains met with skepticism. To remedy this, we present open-data, large-scale, and varied morphological evidence that Xenopus laevis embryo development is accelerated in a well-engineered, environ...
1
Clarice D. Aiello 11/18/2024 8:13 AM
One of the cooler realizations of #DeSci, for me, @pyramidmaker , is that science is not the monopoly of academic labs

Chan 12/5/2024 4:03 AM
The Dutch auction method does not allow investors to have much profit margin, but the risk is very high I think this mechanism represents the greed of the fundraisers. Because the cost of investors will increase over time, and the profit margin will become smaller and smaller as the auction continues, but it is good for the project owners because they can make a lot of money,But they don't care how high the cost to investors is, they only care about how much money they raise.
Chan
The Dutch auction method does not allow investors to have much profit margin, but the risk is very high I think this mechanism represents the greed of the fundraisers. Because the cost of investors will increase over time, and the profit margin will become smaller and smaller as the auction continues, but it is good for the project owners because they can make a lot of money,But they don't care how high the cost to investors is, they only care about how much money they raise.
Loid Mong 12/5/2024 4:27 AM
this is especially concerning as this is the first launch after desci started getting more attention. If market starts perceiving there to not be any upside to be captured from being early, people will simply move over to other sectors. If these first launches are botched from maximally extractive raising, desci is at a risk of being forgotten as quickly as it came to spotlight. Funding science ≠ donating to science
Loid Mong
this is especially concerning as this is the first launch after desci started getting more attention. 2:37 AM

2:38 AM
i’m just curious how they do this
Chan 12/11/2024 3:54 AM
Taking the disci track as an example, many projects add liquidity before the release of tokens.Take the nearest time distance, $cryorat、$ab4ad ....There is no project I know of that does not add a pool first and then release tokens. instead of deciding by voting after the tokens are released, these small projects all know to add liquidity before releasing tokens. scagria
i'm talking about real DAOs where decisions are made by token holders, not projects managed by 2-3 ppl with "DAO" title in their name

Hi @everyone, happy holidays from the team! The core team is meeting early next week to cook up a proposal for QBIO trading. Please bear with us as we expand our comms. In the new year, we expect to continue with our weekly DAO Community Calls on Thu 9am PT (it's marked as an event in the Discord). Talk soon!
Originally sent: 12/27/2024 11:31 AM

Y P 12/27/2024 11:54 AM
https://x.com/bioprotocol/status/1872605221723279380
BIO Protocol (@bioprotocol) on X
"Scientists are the world's most important content creators. We are here to help and give them the tools to get access to these kinds of opportunities." @jamessinka highlights the importance of DeSci funding and how @QuantumBioDAO's auction raised $6.8M in ETH just recently.
Twitter • 12/27/2024 6:27 AM
5
Clarice D. Aiello 12/30/2024 8:01 PM
Forwarded
@everyone 1) The DAO core team will use this discord channel #📢︱governance-announcements to communicate on DAO governance. 2) Governance will be discussed by all in the #📝︱governance-proposals channel. 3) The core team met today Dec 30; 3A) We are putting together a governance proposal -- we are aiming to put it live in Discord this weak, in this channel, for discussion in channel #📝︱governance-proposals. (We are doing our best, but there might be delays since team members are preparing the lab to move.) Following the governance proposal, there are 3 more proposals that will soon follow, in the below order: 3B) Liquidity proposal -- The team is thinking about timing: this proposal should not occur before BIO launch on Binance on Jan 3; we are meeting with BIO team early next week to discuss details of this liquidity proposal. 3C) Working groups proposal (as per successful examples from other BIO DAOs). 3D) Proposal for initial funding allocation. Happy new year to everyone, and talk more soon.
Originally sent: 12/30/2024 8:01 PM
Clarice D. Aiello
Click to see original message

Clarice D. Aiello 1/4/2025 6:51 PM
Forwarded
In exciting news, our lab has moved yesterday... but now the DAO has decisions to make! We're happy to announce the Quantum Biology DAO governance proposal, https://docs.google.com/document/d/1K3yQUMHeIllRyxl4OIpZYQXy9qLo9Y_R7bN2SVOLi1M/edit?usp=sharing It was developed based on other DAOs; and we added provisions to be able to make decisions quickly. Before we have a governance proposal in place, there is no official agreement on the voting mechanism -- but we will be using the voting mechanism in the proposal. So: please read the proposal, leave comments in the google doc, and discuss away in the #📝︱governance-proposals channel!

Clarice D. Aiello 1/11/2025 2:53 PM
Forwarded
@everyone QBIO Liquidity Proposal V 1.1 in Snapshot received more than 3% of circulating QBIO tokens votes, all in favor, no vote against, so that it passed at 11.50am PT on Jan 11 and thus becomes official! You can find a copy of the proposal in ⁠︱passed-proposals. Note: The association thus has 7 days to make QBIO liquid; the clock starts at 11.50am PT on Jan 11. Note: The proposal was marked fastest, meaning it would spend 1 day in Snapshot. When setting up the vote in the website, I made a mistake and set the vote to 3 days, with no possibility of correction. Since communication everywhere was that the poll would be open for 1 day, we will take the results after 1 day as valid. For proof, see attached png image.
Originally sent: 1/11/2025 2:53 PM

Clarice D. Aiello 1/16/2025 11:55 AM
Forwarded
@everyone Reminder that at 9am PT on Thu Jan 16 (in 10 min!) we are going to have our usual Weekly DAO Community Call, in zoom room 617 7170 5261 (https://zoom.us/j/6177105261). The call today is going to be important: Some people have been in favor of making QBIO tradable as per the passed proposal, i.e., by 11.50am PT on Sat 18 at the latest; some people have expressed concerns given the current market situation. Right now, by default, we are going to execute on the resolution that was passed; But there is still time to modify that resolution -- there is a very short window in which we could propose an amendment. Come discuss with us today!

Clarice D. Aiello 1/16/2025 2:53 PM
Forwarded
@everyone Reminder that at 9am PT on Thu Jan 16 (in 10 min!) we are going to have our usual Weekly DAO Community Call, in zoom room 617 7170 5261 (https://zoom.us/j/6177105261). The call today is going to be important: Some people have been in favor of making QBIO tradable as per the passed proposal, i.e., by 11.50am PT on Sat 18 at the latest; some people have expressed concerns given the current market situation. Right now, by default, we are going to execute on the resolution that was passed; But there is still time to modify that resolution -- there is a very short window in which we could propose an amendment. Come discuss with us today!
Originally sent: 1/16/2025 11:54 AM
2:54 PM
Forwarded
@everyone : We're looking forward to the token becoming tradable ASAP and it's currently scheduled to become tradable by Saturday Jan 18, 11:50am PT, at the latest. HOWEVER, a bunch of advisors (including from BIO, but not only from BIO) have suggested that market conditions are not good for a launch right now. We propose an amendment that will give the team latitude to launch the token when they judge that market conditions permit. We are proposing this NOT because we are in favor of waiting or not waiting (we are actually very excited to get the token tradable) but because we received these warnings and wanted to make it possible to change course if that's what people want to do. So: please read the proposal, leave comments in the google doc, and discuss away in the ⁠︱governance-forum channel! https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kveWidGmO6RxEpGn1O3gN9GZLhBYfgeKmkjSTX9dIW0/edit?usp=sharing Once it gets 5 in its thread in ⁠︱governance-forum , it will be moved to Phase 2 in Commonwealth with speed fastest (1 day in Commonwealth, as per the new proposal nomenclature).

Clarice D. Aiello 1/29/2025 6:57 PM
Forwarded
@everyone We're happy to announce the Quantum Biology Institute Tideover Budget for Feb 25, V 1.0, https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YRyxyHCcX6c-O39VOjWsLG3RWpFCtNYkRjbbdd9GhZU/edit?usp=sharing The proposal provides a budget to cover the Quantum Biology Institute operations for the month of Feb 25, while its research infrastructure is being established. We expect to make a proposal for a full budget for the Institute in the coming weeks. This proposal will advance from Phase 1 through 3 following the rules just established by the recently passed Governance Proposal V 1.1 (for a copy, check ⁠︱passed-proposals ). So: please read the proposal, leave comments in the google doc, and discuss away in the ⁠︱governance-forum channel! Once it gets 5 in its thread in ⁠︱governance-forum , it can be moved to Phase 2 in Commonwealth with speed fastest (1 day in Commonwealth). Note: this one is the last proposal expected to be passed at speed fastest for now; our plan is to move to normal speed for the next proposals, which will include a budget for the DAO and the full budget proposal for the Institute.
Originally sent: 1/29/2025 6:56 PM
Clarice D. Aiello 1/29/2025 7:23 PM
Forwarded
@everyone The Quantum Biology Institute Tideover Budget for Feb 2025 Proposal V 1.0 got at least 5 upvotes in ⁠︱governance-forum, including 2 core DAO members, at 4:20pm PT on Jan 29 2025, fulfilling the requirements for Phase 1. It is now available in Commonwealth for Phase 2, with speed fastest, meaning it will be open for comments for 1 day. Hence, Commonwealth thread comments and votes will be taken into account if posted before 4:20pm PT on Thu Jan 30 2025. Link to Commonwealth proposal: https://common.xyz/quantum-biology-dao/discussion/26322-Discussion%20about%20Quantum%20Biology%20Institute%20Tideover%20Budget%20for%20Feb%2025%20Proposal%20V%201.0 The Quantum Biology DAO Commonwealth community is found at: https://common.xyz/quantum-biology-dao/discussions?tab=all Note, bis: this one is the last proposal expected to be passed at speed fastest for now; our plan is to move to normal speed for the next proposals, which will include a budget for the DAO and the full budget proposal for the Institute.
Originally sent: 1/29/2025 7:23 PM

Geoff 1/29/2025 9:10 PM

9:12 PM
I've spent some time trying to figure out how much it's possible to understand what's happening with the token price. I've had these three thoughts: (1) there is natural slow growth, but this was interrupted by the big buy which caused people to think the token was overvalued (2) there is natural slow decline, because people are excited about meme coins and $TRUMP and so forth, where the energy is going over there rather than into DeSci (3) this stuff is completely unpredictable Obviously I don't know which is right. People have been chattering about DeSci being in a slump, but I think DeSci is still just getting started.
3

Alan 7/29/2025 12:10 PM
@Geoff Hi Geoff, I’ve been exploring Quantum Biology DAO and couldn’t find much detail about the QBIO token’s utility or governance role on the official site. Could you clarify how QBIO is intended to function within the DAO ecosystem — particularly in terms of funding research, voting, or incentivizing participation? Also, could you share the tokenomics breakdown? For example, how is the 1 billion QBIO supply distributed across the core team, treasury, service providers, and community incentives? I also noticed the ‘Get QBIO Token’ button redirects to Discord, which felt unexpected. It gives the impression that the token is being promoted without a clear, accessible explanation of its fundamentals. I’d really appreciate more transparency around its purpose, distribution, and how it supports the DAO’s mission. Lastly, I understand your strength lies in pioneering quantum biology itself, but I wonder who within the DAO is responsible for the financial architecture and token strategy. Is there a dedicated financial lead or manager who could speak to the economic layer of the project?”
Alan
@Geoff Hi Geoff, I’ve been exploring Quantum Biology DAO and couldn’t find much detail about the QBIO token’s utility or governance role on the official site. Could you clarify how QBIO is intended to function within the DAO ecosystem — particularly in terms of funding research, voting, or incentivizing participation? Also, could you share the tokenomics breakdown? For example, how is the 1 billion QBIO supply distributed across the core team, treasury, service providers, and community incentives? I also noticed the ‘Get QBIO Token’ button redirects to Discord, which felt unexpected. It gives the impression that the token is being promoted without a clear, accessible explanation of its fundamentals. I’d really appreciate more transparency around its purpose, distribution, and how it supports the DAO’s mission. Lastly, I understand your strength lies in pioneering quantum biology itself, but I wonder who within the DAO is responsible for the financial architecture and token strategy. Is there a dedicated financial lead or manager who could speak to the economic layer of the project?”
Cryptonurse 7/29/2025 1:33 PM
Hi @Alan thanks for rising this questions while geoff is busy at the moment but i know he will reply when he saw the message.before then i can explain some of the questions you asked. Regards to QBIO utility and other information you can easily find the information in the QBIODAO Vision paper you can access the docs here https://bit.ly/qbiodaovision And also we are working on updating the website to the best version and all other features will be included. For governance we have governance channel where all governance matter is being discussed https://discord.com/channels/1248368139436163143/1265784747137830983/1395896284950565026 https://discord.com/channels/1248368139436163143/1372287911093076078/1385994371069120744 https://discord.com/channels/1248368139436163143/1399631368358461502 and we are using Commonwealth and snapshot for governance voting you can find the passed governance proposal in the Qbidao page at Commonwealth here https://common.xyz/quantum-biology-dao/discussions?tab=all The Qbi roadmap is clearly stated at qbiodao docs with the distribution of: Treasury = 55% Community Auction = 15% Community incentives = 3% Service providers = 7% Core team = 20% (edited)
20241205 V1.2 Quantum Biology DAO Vision Paper.pdf
Common
Discuss, organize, and grow decentralized communities
Alan
@Geoff Hi Geoff, I’ve been exploring Quantum Biology DAO and couldn’t find much detail about the QBIO token’s utility or governance role on the official site. Could you clarify how QBIO is intended to function within the DAO ecosystem — particularly in terms of funding research, voting, or incentivizing participation? Also, could you share the tokenomics breakdown? For example, how is the 1 billion QBIO supply distributed across the core team, treasury, service providers, and community incentives? I also noticed the ‘Get QBIO Token’ button redirects to Discord, which felt unexpected. It gives the impression that the token is being promoted without a clear, accessible explanation of its fundamentals. I’d really appreciate more transparency around its purpose, distribution, and how it supports the DAO’s mission. Lastly, I understand your strength lies in pioneering quantum biology itself, but I wonder who within the DAO is responsible for the financial architecture and token strategy. Is there a dedicated financial lead or manager who could speak to the economic layer of the project?”
Geoff 7/29/2025 7:44 PM
Hi @Alan, happy to answer. (1) QBIO is a governance token. People use it to vote on proposals, which can include funding research. In terms of the details, the DAO has a three phase governance system. The first phase happens here, on Discord, where proposals are drafted. Then they are voted on in Common.xyz, where you need 10,000 QBIO in order to vote. Finally, there is a token-weighted vote (1 token, 1 vote) in Snapshot. • https://common.xyz/quantum-biology-dao/ • https://snapshot.box/#/s:qbio.eth
7:44 PM
(2) For community incentives, community contributors can elect to receive rewards in QBIO. We give out community incentives each quarter.
7:44 PM
(3) The tokenomics breakdown is on p. 9 in the original vision paper: • https://bit.ly/qbiodaovision
20241205 V1.2 Quantum Biology DAO Vision Paper.pdf
7:44 PM
Thanks for pointing out the "Get QBIO Token" button issue, that's an error. We're revising the website right now. I'm sorry if that part was confusing.
7:45 PM
In terms of who is responsible for financial architecture and token strategy, that would be Mike Montague (@montague.archimedesnetwork_96844) as the head of the Treasury working group, and then the Core Contributors of the DAO, which are currently myself (@Geoff), @alessandrolodesani, and @Clarice D. Aiello.
1
7:45 PM
Please feel free to ask any other questions you have, like I said, we're updating the website now and want to have all of this information available for people.
1

beverlythesheep 10/5/2025 11:44 AM
Hi Shepherd @Geoff I'm confused as to how donating $1M to a Quantum Biology charity is going to help our DAO or treasury to grow. Thank you sir!
beverlythesheep
Hi Shepherd @Geoff I'm confused as to how donating $1M to a Quantum Biology charity is going to help our DAO or treasury to grow. Thank you sir!
Geoff 10/7/2025 11:24 AM
The purpose of the DAO is to advance the field of quantum biology, from the basic science through to the development of an industry. The first part of that is getting a bunch of the basic science done, which requires being able to experimentally probe quantum states. That's the reason for that. In terms of how the DAO treasury will grow, we have a bunch of QBIO in the treasury. As interest in quantum biology grows — which comes from advances in basic science and promotion of the field — the perceived value of the field will go up, which will increase (I assume) the value of the ability to govern the field. That increases the value of governance tokens, which the DAO can then sell to raise more money to advance the field. Does that help?
1
1
Geoff
The purpose of the DAO is to advance the field of quantum biology, from the basic science through to the development of an industry. The first part of that is getting a bunch of the basic science done, which requires being able to experimentally probe quantum states. That's the reason for that. In terms of how the DAO treasury will grow, we have a bunch of QBIO in the treasury. As interest in quantum biology grows — which comes from advances in basic science and promotion of the field — the perceived value of the field will go up, which will increase (I assume) the value of the ability to govern the field. That increases the value of governance tokens, which the DAO can then sell to raise more money to advance the field. Does that help?

Geoff 10/10/2025 12:31 PM
Hi @beverlythesheep, point well-taken on the watering of the grass. One thing we're looking at is releasing an instrument token that people can buy to license instrument IP the DAO is developing. Proceeds from that could then be split between going to the DAO and buying and burning instrument token. We don't have a name for the instrument token yet, but maybe QINST. In terms of IP, @alessandrolodesani suggested we might have a prototype of an adjustable magnetic ring (for applying uniform magnetic fields to biological samples) next week. We believe that that will be patentable (and that basically no one has patented a lot of instruments in this area). So, that plan will give the DAO genuine IP, which then has a real use (can be licensed by groups that want to 3-D print their own versions), and revenue from which would feed into the DAO and reduce the supply of QINST. How's that? (edited)
2
Geoff
Hi @beverlythesheep, point well-taken on the watering of the grass. One thing we're looking at is releasing an instrument token that people can buy to license instrument IP the DAO is developing. Proceeds from that could then be split between going to the DAO and buying and burning instrument token. We don't have a name for the instrument token yet, but maybe QINST. In terms of IP, @alessandrolodesani suggested we might have a prototype of an adjustable magnetic ring (for applying uniform magnetic fields to biological samples) next week. We believe that that will be patentable (and that basically no one has patented a lot of instruments in this area). So, that plan will give the DAO genuine IP, which then has a real use (can be licensed by groups that want to 3-D print their own versions), and revenue from which would feed into the DAO and reduce the supply of QINST. How's that? (edited)
beverlythesheep 10/12/2025 7:23 AM
Hi Shepherd, as a sheep who has grazed on many, many pastures, what I can report from the various fields is I've never seen one work with two tokens. Not one. Never. Will your 1.5M flock be different? Is innovating the problem, or is it utility? Shepherd, we haven't even planted all our seeds and grown a pasture of our own and you're trying to build another pasture? I'm afraid the few sheep we have won't know where to graze. Sheep, I'm sure you'll appreciate, have limited capacities. They can barely understand one token, and you want them to learn about two? Grass will be found elsewhere. What's stopping you, my Shepherd, from giving $QBIO all the utility it needs by putting all future IPs under our already existing token?
1
Geoff 10/13/2025 11:54 AM
Greetings, O @beverlythesheep, your words continue to be of interest. The "two tokens never work" point is a good one and you may be right. Would you count UNUS SEO LEO as a second token attached to Tether? Or the IPT tokens launched by VitaDAO? Permit my ignorance, I do seek to learn.
1
Geoff
Greetings, O @beverlythesheep, your words continue to be of interest. The "two tokens never work" point is a good one and you may be right. Would you count UNUS SEO LEO as a second token attached to Tether? Or the IPT tokens launched by VitaDAO? Permit my ignorance, I do seek to learn.
beverlythesheep 10/14/2025 8:35 AM
We don't even have a single functioning token, so there's no reason to create a second one. We need to give the first one utility first. What can the second token do that the first can't? Why complicate what can be simple? Why expend resources building, creating and explaining a new token model? Is that really the problem which hinders our growth at this stage? We have other projects with 10B+ MC with a single token, whereas there are very few cases of two token projects working term (perhaps 1%). Vita is unique because they are tokenizing an individual IP and bringing that value back to VITA, but I think with a small project like QBIO, all the IP profits can flow back into QBIO in the form of a buyback anyway, so it just keeps it very simple and clean. Fewer decisions for sheep and more comfort knowing the Shepherd is tending to the grass. Lest not forget who the average grazer is, my dear Shepherd.
1
Geoff 10/14/2025 9:22 AM
@beverlythesheep I will bring your arguments to the community at our Thursday meeting (12pm ET). Feel free to attend also to make sure they are argued well. I am more or less convinced but would like to get more feedback from people.
2
1
1
Geoff 10/15/2025 9:31 AM

Geoff

beverlythesheep 10/15/2025 3:56 PM
Ah, governance. Some sheep can be led to believe they are the Shepherd by voting to direct the herd. But I ask you to ponder my Shepherd, while you are grazing in the green pastures from which you frequent: why should us sheep care about voting where the heard goes and the grass grows, if we cannot eat the grass ourselves?
beverlythesheep
Ah, governance. Some sheep can be led to believe they are the Shepherd by voting to direct the herd. But I ask you to ponder my Shepherd, while you are grazing in the green pastures from which you frequent: why should us sheep care about voting where the heard goes and the grass grows, if we cannot eat the grass ourselves?
Geoff 10/17/2025 10:36 AM
The sheep ought eat from the same pastures as the shepherd, so to speak — though even with real sheep and shepherds there is the question of who leads who. In any event, your idea was accepted unanimously by those present who spoke up, which included myself, @alessandrolodesani, @Kyra, @Jennilyn Gaitan, @Cryptonurse, and perhaps others. It therefore seems that we will pack all of the utility into the QBIO token and revisit the idea of splitting out a token if having all the utility in QBIO becomes too complicated. The community is grateful, O Sheep. We hope you will frequent this area in the future .
